meta.description: Arabic sorting — sort key generation for consistent ordering across implementations
meta.notes: This library provides sort keys, not a full Unicode Collation Algorithm. Sort keys are derived via toSearchKey, with additional rules for letter-order consistency. The goal is: words that 'mean the same' sort together, regardless of hamza/alef/tashkeel variants.

cases.name: Sort key for name with hamza-above alef
Sort key for name with alef maqsoura
Sort key for ta marbouta name — ة sorts as ة for sort purposes
cases.notes: In sort key, ة is preserved. We do NOT convert ة→ه for sort. فاطمة and فاطمه would be different sort keys — this is intentional. Applications that need them to group must call normalizeTaMarbouta explicitly.

cases.name: Equivalence: أحمد and احمد should produce same sort key
cases.notes: Both أحمد (with hamza) and احمد (bare alef) should sort identically

cases.name: Equivalence: مُحَمَّد and محمد should produce same sort key
Sort a list of Arabic names — returns original strings in sorted order
cases.notes: v1 sort is stable lexical ordering over toSortKey output. Sort keys at discriminating position: ابراهيم(ب=U+0628) < احمد(ح=U+062D) < ادم(د=U+062F). This is NOT full Arabic dictionary collation (which requires locale-aware collation rules). Full collation may be added as ArabicCollator in v2.

cases.name: toSortKey for each name in the list — returns normalized keys
cases.notes: toSortKey() applies to each element independently. Returns normalized keys, not original strings.

cases.name: Sort names with alef maqsoura variants together
cases.notes: Both map to same sort key 'موسي' — original order preserved for equal keys (stable sort)

cases.name: Sort list of Arabic cities — returns original strings
cases.notes: v1 sort is stable lexical ordering over toSortKey output. Sort keys at discriminating position: الاسكندرية(ا=U+0627) < الرياض(ر=U+0631) < القاهرة(ق=U+0642). The 'ال' prefix is kept — no definite article stripping. This is NOT full Arabic dictionary collation (which requires locale-aware collation rules). Full collation may be added as ArabicCollator in v2.

cases.name: compare: أحمد vs احمد — equal (0)
cases.notes: compare(a, b) returns -1, 0, or 1 like a standard comparator
